title: Bruins’ prospect James Hagens turns pro, will begin career in Providence
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: James Hagens signs AHL amateur tryout agreement, leaving Boston College to join the Providence Bruins.
Hagens, the Bruins' top prospect, recorded 47 points in 34 games during his sophomore year at Boston College.
Hagens was the seventh overall pick in the 2025 NHL Draft, viewed as a key future asset for the Bruins.

title: 7 X-factors on 2026 Red Sox roster
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Marcelo Mayer is expected to improve Boston's infield with athletic defense and potential power despite past struggles and durability concerns.
Roman Anthony, with only 71 MLB games, is seen as a potential MVP, leading the lineup as Boston aims for a strong season.
Brayan Bello and Connelly Early are key to strengthening Boston's already promising rotation for success in the 2026 season.

title: State House Minority Leader opts not to run again after 32 years in office
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Bradley H. Jones, Jr., House Minority Leader, will not seek reelection after 32 years in the Massachusetts State House.
Jones has represented the 20th Middlesex District since 1994, covering Lynnfield, Middleton, North Reading, and Reading.
Amy Carnevale, Massachusetts Republican Party chair, commended Jones for his dedication and integrity during his tenure.

title: No ICE agents at Logan Airport yet as agents deploy to busy airports
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: No ICE agents have been deployed to New England airports yet, including Logan Airport in Boston.
Homeland Security confirmed a TSA operation at Logan but did not deploy ICE agents as reported by Massport.
ICE agents were sent to 14 airports due to long wait times but Logan Airport avoided such delays.

title: Fitchburg fire chief on paid leave pending external investigation, mayor says
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: Fitchburg Fire Chief Dante Suarez has been on paid leave since February 26 pending an external investigation.
Assistant Chief Patrick Haverty is serving as acting chief following Suarez's leave, confirmed by Mayor Sam Squailia.
Details regarding Chief Suarez's leave were not disclosed due to personnel matters, according to city officials.

title: Person seriously injured in Seaport stabbing
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: A man was seriously injured after being stabbed in the abdomen and leg in Boston's Seaport district on Sunday night.
The stabbing was reported outside 72 Seaport Blvd at 2:44 a.m Sunday; the victim was found near Kings Dining and Entertainment.
Police have not identified any suspects or made arrests; the investigation into the incident is ongoing.

title: Here’s what happened during the first day of Kelsey Fitzsimmons’s trial
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Kelsey Fitzsimmons's bench trial began Monday in Essex County Superior Court with opening statements from both prosecution and defense.
Prosecutors argue Fitzsimmons aimed a gun at Officer Patrick Noonan, prompting him to shoot her in self-defense.
The defense claims Fitzsimmons intended to harm herself, not Noonan, and was shot due to a misunderstanding.

title: Bruins host Blackstone Valley co-op hockey team at practice after run to R.I. state title ​
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: The Bruins hosted the Blackstone Valley co-op hockey team at Warrior Ice Arena after their Rhode Island Division II State Championship win.
Blackstone Valley players interacted with Bruins stars, including Jeremy Swayman, amid recent community adversity and triumphs.
The Knights' championship run was emotional, highlighted by Colin Dorgan's critical goals following a personal family tragedy.

title: Patriots sign offensive tackle James Hudson
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Patriots sign offensive tackle James Hudson, released by the Giants, adding depth to their roster.
Hudson has appeared in 60 NFL games with 19 starts, mainly at right tackle, entering his sixth season.
Hudson offers experience at left tackle, potentially backing up Morgan Moses and compensating for other roster changes.

title: As waiting game continues with James Hagens, Bruins coach has no issue adding prospects to lineup
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: James Hagens, Bruins' top prospect, has not yet signed a pro deal after his sophomore season at Boston College.
Hagens recorded 47 points in 34 games this season, raising speculation about his transition to professional hockey.
Bruins coach Marco Sturm is open to integrating young players like Hagens into the lineup if they're up to the task.
